tool.name: GaCDI Manifest Builder (PDC)
tool.description: build a filtered PDC download manifest (DRS) and enriched metadata table
tool.command: gacdi-manifest pdc #if str($core.pdc_study_id).strip() != '' --pdc-study-id '$core.pdc_study_id' #end if #if str($core.disease_type).strip() != '' --disease-type '$core.disease_type' #end if #if str($core.primary_site).strip() != '' --primary-site '$core.primary_site' #end if #if str($core.analytical_fraction).strip() != '' --analytical-fraction '$core.analytical_fraction' #end if #if str($core.data_category).strip() != '' --data-category '$core.data_category' #end if #for $mf in $adv.metadata_filters --metadata-filter 'column=${mf.column};op=${mf.op};values=${mf.values}' #end for #if $count_only --count-only #else #if str($adv.max_files) != '' --max-files $adv.max_files #end if #end if --manifest-out '$manifest' --metadata-out '$metadata' --report-out '$report' --selection-manifest-out '$selection_manifest' --selection-metadata-out '$selection_metadata' --selection-provenance-out '$selection_provenance'

param.name: pdc_study_id
param.label: PDC study id(s)
param.help: One or more PDC study ids (comma-separated), e.g. PDC000714. Leave blank to select studies by the filters below.

param.name: disease_type
param.label: Disease type
param.help: Keep only studies of this disease, e.g. Colon Adenocarcinoma. Comma-separate for several.

param.name: primary_site
param.label: Primary site
param.help: Keep only studies from this anatomic site, e.g. Brain, Colon. Comma-separate for several.

param.name: analytical_fraction
param.label: Analytical fraction
param.help: Proteomics fraction, e.g. Proteome, Phosphoproteome, Ubiquitylome. Comma-separate for several.

param.name: data_category
param.label: Data category
param.help: Keep only files in this category, e.g. 'Raw Mass Spectra' or 'Processed Mass Spectra'.

param.name: count_only
param.label: Preview counts only (don't build the manifest yet)
param.help: Reports how many files match so you can refine filters before building the full manifest.

repeat.name: metadata_filters
repeat.title: Filter the final table by metadata column
repeat.help: Applied AFTER the query, on the metadata columns this tool generated (headers in the metadata output — e.g. data_category, or a native column like pdc__analytical_fraction). Keeps only rows where a column is present or matches specific value(s). A file stays in the manifest if at least one of its rows passes.

param.name: column
param.label: Metadata column
param.help: Exact column header from the metadata output, e.g. disease_type, primary_site, pdc__analytical_fraction.

param.name: op
param.label: Condition
param.help: How to test each row's value.

param.name: values
param.label: Value(s)
param.help: Comma-separated. Required for 'is one of', 'is not', and 'contains'; leave blank for 'is present' / 'is blank'.

param.name: max_files
param.label: Maximum number of files
param.help: Stop after this many files — handy for a quick test. Leave blank for all matches.

tool.help:
**GaCDI Manifest Builder (PDC)**
Builds a **targeted** download manifest from the NCI `Proteomic Data Commons <https://proteomic.datacommons.cancer.gov/>`_ (PDC), a CRDC node, so you fetch exactly the proteomics files you want. It produces three compatibility outputs plus the canonical three-file selection bundle:
1. **PDC DRS manifest** — the multi-source download contract (``source``, ``file_id``, ``drs_uri``, ``download_method``, ``checksum``, ``size``, ``access`` …). Every row is a GA4GH **DRS** URI (``drs://dg.4DFC:<id>``) resolved by the CRDC DRS server, so the downstream downloader fetches by DRS. 2. **metadata** — a research table: a harmonized core (``disease_type``, ``primary_site``, ``project`` …) plus every native PDC field preserved as ``pdc__`` prefixed columns. 3. **report** — match counts, provenance and (in preview mode) the match count. 4. **canonical selection manifest / metadata / provenance** — the validated, hash-bound source-neutral bundle reserved for the future PDC DRS downloader.
**Getting started**
Give a **PDC study id** (e.g. ``PDC000714``) or pick studies by *Disease type* / *Primary site* / *Analytical fraction*, and run with *Preview counts only* first. When the count looks right, turn preview off to build the manifest.
**Two kinds of filter.** The *What to download* fields filter which PDC studies/files the query returns. The Advanced *Filter the final table by metadata column* trims the final manifest to rows whose generated columns match (e.g. keep only a given ``data_category``), after the query runs.
